hypothesis: Incorporating molecular charge as a continuous input feature to MLIP models trained on the curated SPICE2 dataset—rather than simply excluding non-neutral structures—will enable accurate force and energy predictions across a range of ionic and zwitterionic species (including protonated/deprotonated drug-like molecules and charged amino acid residues) without degrading MD simulation stability on neutral systems, as measured by force MAE, energy MAE, and simulation drift metrics compared to the charge-agnostic baseline models presented here.
